question: Does the output include illustrated explanations for all four requested perspective types: One-Point, Two-Point, Three-Point, and Fisheye/Curvilinear?
Does the output generate a distinct architectural scene image for each of the four perspective types (no duplicate images or abstract non-architectural diagrams)?
Does the text explicitly explain the specific composition suitability for each linear perspective type (e.g., One-Point for tunnels/focus, Two-Point for corners, Three-Point for extreme height)?
Does the text explanation for Fisheye perspective correctly identify the essential difference from linear perspective (e.g., projection onto a curved/spherical surface vs. a flat plane)?
Does the One-Point perspective image accurately depict a scene with exactly one vanishing point located on the horizon line?
In the One-Point perspective image, do the front-facing vertical and horizontal lines remain parallel (non-converging), with only depth lines converging?
Does the Two-Point perspective image accurately depict a scene with exactly two vanishing points located on the horizon line?
In the Two-Point perspective image, do the vertical architectural lines remain perpendicular to the ground (non-converging), distinguishing it from Three-Point perspective?
Does the Three-Point perspective image accurately depict a scene with three vanishing points (two on the horizon, one vertical for height/depth)?
In the Three-Point perspective image, do the vertical lines converge toward a third vanishing point (above or below), indicating a tilted camera view?
Does the Fisheye perspective image visually demonstrate barrel distortion where straight architectural lines appear curved, clearly distinguishing it from the linear examples?
Are the specific visual elements 'Vanishing Point(s)' and 'Horizon Line' explicitly marked or labeled in the generated linear perspective images?
Are the labels (e.g., VP, Horizon) within the generated images legible and clearly distinguishing construction elements from the architectural scene?
Do the architectural scenes depicted in the images semantically match the descriptions provided in the surrounding text (e.g., matching the described building type or viewpoint)?
Are the images placed immediately adjacent to their corresponding theoretical explanations to serve as direct visual proofs?
Is the content structured in a logical pedagogical progression (e.g., One-Point -> Two-Point -> Three-Point -> Fisheye)?
Are the perspective constructions in the images internally self-consistent (e.g., all parallel edges converge to the same VP without conflicting angles)?
Does the output avoid factual errors, such as claiming Two-Point perspective has vertical convergence or that Linear perspective curves straight lines?
In the Two-Point perspective image, do the two sets of horizontal receding lines converge strictly to their respective separate vanishing points without cross-contamination?
